task_completion.description: Verify that the three required layers ('Treads', 'Labels', 'Dimensions') have been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Layer List panel on the right side of the LibreCAD interface. You should see layers named 'Treads', 'Labels', and 'Dimensions' in addition to the default layer '0'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the staircase geometry is drawn with exactly 10 treads starting at the origin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the main drawing area. There should be a rectangular outline with 10 equal horizontal subdivisions (11 horizontal lines in total), representing the treads of a staircase. The bottom-left corner of the staircase should align with the red cross indicating the origin (0,0).

task_completion.description: Verify that the risers are numbered 1 through 10 and an 'UP' indicator with an arrow is present.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for numbers '1' through '10' placed inside each of the 10 treads. Also look for the text 'UP' accompanied by an arrow pointing upwards (+Y direction, along the length of the stairs).

task_completion.description: Verify that linear dimensions for the stair width and total run are included.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for dimension lines outside the staircase geometry. There should be a dimension showing the width as '900' and a dimension showing the total run (length) as '2500'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the drawing is saved as 'staircase_plan.dxf'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the title bar of the LibreCAD window or the command line history at the bottom. The filename 'staircase_plan.dxf' should be visible, indicating the file was saved to the correct location.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the LibreCAD GUI to create the drawing.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the LibreCAD application window with drawing tools, layer lists, and command line being actively used, rather than just a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not just run a pre-written script to generate the DXF file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The command line history in LibreCAD should show individual commands for drawing lines, adding text, and dimensions, rather than a single script execution or no history at all.

integrity.description: Verify that the geometry and text are actually drawn in the workspace.
integrity.visual_evidence: The drawing area should display the CAD elements (lines, text, dimensions) matching the LibreCAD rendering style, not an imported image or pasted text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The staircase must have exactly 10 treads (11 horizontal lines) with a total run dimension of 2500 and a width dimension of 900. The layers must be named 'Treads', 'Labels', and 'Dimensions'. The text labels must include numbers 1 through 10 and the word 'UP' with an upward-pointing arrow. The final file must be saved as 'staircase_plan.dxf'.
